mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicate synchronized demand recovery, which boosts cyclical earnings and lifts S&P 500 prices within one month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in HOUST and RSAFS do not produce positive SPX 20-day returns in at least 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating inflation, the Federal Reserve's reactive rate hikes compress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly SPX forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates above 3.0% annually, economic momentum validates corporate earnings upgrades, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market strength, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: Joint positive PAYEMS and negative UNRATE changes fail to produce positive SPX 5-day returns in at least 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 2.5% and housing starts decline, stagflationary pressures compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months meeting both inflation and housing decline criteria.
